candidate.title: Formal invariant property suite for all three circuits
candidate.description: Write machine-checkable invariant properties covering: (1) no field overflow — all uint64 signals stay within [0, 2^64) after range checks, (2) nullifier uniqueness — for any two distinct (secret, scope) or (credCommitment, nonce) pairs the nullifier outputs differ, (3) scope monotonicity — in Delegation, delegateeScope AND NOT delegatorScope == 0 for all valid witnesses, (4) expiry narrowing — delegateeExpiry <= delegatorExpiry. Deliver as a property specification file (Certora CVL or equivalent assertion harness in circom_tester) with at least 15 named properties and a CI-runnable test script.

candidate.title: Conformance Test Suite with JSON Test Vectors
candidate.description: Produce 50+ JSON test vectors covering all three circuits and the delegation chain. Each vector specifies: circuit name, input signals (valid or invalid), expected output signals or expected rejection reason. Categories: valid handshake (human+agent), expired credential (currentTimestamp > expiryTimestamp), revoked human nullifier, stale Merkle root (not in 30-root buffer), scope subset violation in delegation, cumulative bit encoding violation (bit 4 without bits 2+3), delegation chain at depth 1/3/4 (4 must fail), and cross-hop scope commitment mismatch. Ship as a standalone JSON file with a JSON Schema for the vector format, enabling any implementation to run the suite without snarkjs.

candidate.title: Cross-chain test vectors for conformance suite
candidate.description: Extend the planned conformance test suite with 10+ cross-chain-specific JSON test vectors covering: proof generated on chain A verified on chain B (should fail without chain ID binding), stale root after relay delay, storage proof verification with valid/invalid block hashes, batch checkpoint inclusion proof, and replay of nullifier across chains. Each vector should include the source chain ID, target chain ID, relay timestamp, and expected verification result. These vectors are essential for any third-party bridge implementer.

candidate.title: Add humanTree root history buffer matching agentTree's 30-root buffer
candidate.description: IdentityRegistry.sol maintains a 30-root history buffer for agentTree but none for humanTree. Any new human enrollment immediately invalidates in-flight HumanUniqueness proofs because the on-chain root changes. This is a liveness bug under load: if humans enroll faster than the proving time (~10-15s for Groth16 on mobile), proofs fail deterministically. Add an identical uint256[30] humanRootHistory buffer with the same ring-buffer logic. No circuit changes needed—only the Solidity root check in verifyHandshake needs to query the buffer instead of a single root. Estimated: 30 lines of Solidity.

candidate.title: W3C DID Method Specification: did:bolyra
candidate.description: Define did:bolyra:<tree>:<commitment> as a W3C DID method per the DID Core v1.0 specification. The DID Document maps identityCommitment (human) and credentialCommitment (agent) to verification methods using the JsonWebKey2020 type with Baby Jubjub curve parameters. Resolution algorithm: given a DID, query the on-chain LeanIMT for membership, return a DID Document with the Merkle root as metadata. Define a VC issuance flow where a successful handshake produces a VerifiableCredential with proof type 'Groth16Proof2024' or 'PlonkProof2024', enabling interop with existing W3C VC wallets and verifiers.

candidate.title: IETF Internet-Draft: Bolyra Core Protocol Specification
candidate.description: Author an IETF Internet-Draft (draft-bolyra-identity-core-00) following RFC 7322 formatting with RFC 2119/8174 requirement keywords. Sections: Introduction, Terminology (human/agent/delegator/delegatee/scope/nullifier), Protocol Overview (3-circuit architecture), Circuit Public Interface Specifications (input/output signal schemas per circuit), Wire Format (CBOR-encoded proof payloads with CDDL schema), On-Chain Verification Interface (ABI specification), and Security Considerations (replay, sybil, scope brute-force, root staleness). This is the foundational document that all other standards work references.

candidate.title: EIP-1186 storage proof cross-chain root sync
candidate.description: Implement cross-chain root synchronization using EIP-1186 storage proofs. Deploy a BolyraRootRelay contract on Arbitrum and Polygon that accepts an RLP-encoded storage proof of IdentityRegistry's humanTree and agentTree roots from Base. The relay contract verifies the proof against a known Base block hash (available via L1 state roots on each L2), then caches the verified roots locally. This eliminates reliance on trusted relayers and lets proofs generated against Base roots be verified on any target chain with ~15 minute latency.

candidate.title: Fluent delegation chain builder API
candidate.description: The Delegation circuit requires manually constructing scope bitmasks, computing credential commitments, and threading previousScopeCommitment between hops. Build a DelegationChain builder with a fluent API: `DelegationChain.from(agentIdentity).narrow({ canRead: true, canWrite: false, financialLimit: '$1k' }).to(delegateeIdentity).expiresIn('1h').build()`. The builder should enforce cumulative bit encoding invariants (bit 4 implies bits 2+3) at construction time with clear errors, serialize the full chain as a portable JSON token, and handle multi-hop threading automatically up to the 3-hop contract limit.
